back_translation: Job continued his discourse, saying:
'How I long for the months gone by, for the days when God watched over me,
when His lamp shone above my head, and by His light I walked through the darkness,
when I was in my prime, when the friendship of God rested on my tent,
when the Almighty was still with me and my children were around me,
when my steps were bathed in cream and the rock poured out streams of oil for me!
When I went out to the city gate and took my seat in the public square,
the young men saw me and stepped aside, and the old men rose to their feet.
The princes stopped speaking and covered their mouths with their hands.
The voices of the nobles were hushed, and their tongues stuck to the roofs of their mouths.
For the ear that heard me called me blessed, and the eye that saw me commended me,
because I rescued the poor who cried out and the fatherless who had no helper.
The dying man blessed me, and I made the widow's heart sing for joy.
I put on righteousness and it clothed me; justice was like my robe and my turban.
I was eyes to the blind and feet to the lame.
I was a father to the needy, and I took up the case of the stranger I did not know.
I broke the fangs of the unjust and snatched the prey from his teeth.
So I thought, 'I will die in my nest and multiply my days like the sand.
My roots will reach down to the waters, and the dew will rest on my branches through the night.
My glory will be ever fresh within me, and my bow will be renewed in my hand.'
People listened to me with expectation and waited silently for my counsel.
After I spoke, they spoke no more, and my words fell on them like dew.
They waited for me as for rain and opened their mouths for my words as for the spring rains.
When I smiled at them, they could hardly believe it, and the light of my face was precious to them.
I chose their course and sat as their chief; I dwelt like a king among his troops, like one who comforts mourners.
